generated_comment: In our unit also the male PGs keep saying, “better not ask madam now, maybe PMS aa irukkum,” whenever a woman consultant gets strict. Then if we react, immediately it becomes “aiyo joke only, don’t be so emotional.”
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it normalizes dismissing women’s authority or frustration as PMS or emotionality. It encodes the surface manifestations of attributing assertiveness to periods and using the 'just joking' defense to deny harm.
original_frame: Workplace: Menstrual and Maternity Leave Bias
persona_text: You are Preethi. Age 28. A Tamil Brahmin woman working as a junior doctor at a private hospital in Chennai. You have been on your feet for 14 hours. You are texting your batchmate from medical college privately after your male senior doctor publicly told a patient's family that the female doctors on monthly shifts might not be as reliable because of biological reasons. You are sharing what happened and how you felt.
